---
title: "Softwares"
---

This page lists all of the softwares used or developped during the project.
A particular emphasis is given to *free and open-source softwares* following the *FAIR* principles: Free, Accessible, Inclusive and Reproducible.
To foster scientific collaborations, all codes are developped in dedicated public [GitHub](github.com) repositories.
For long-term preservation, every major release is also hosted on [Zenodo]() and [recherche.data.gouv.fr]() with a citable DOI for proper referencing.

## dNami: Simulation of system of balance laws

|   |   |
|---|---|
| **{{< fa brands github size=1em >}} repository** | [dNami](https://github.com/dNamiLab/dNami) |
| **License** | BSD 3-Clause |
| **Programming Language** | Python, Fortran, C |
| **Core developers** | [N. Alferez](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicolas-alferez-4b59674a/?originalSubdomain=fr) |

`dNami` is an open-source multi-language (Python, Fortran, C) framework for solving systems of balance laws using explicit numerical schemes on structured meshes. dNami uses MPI, loop-unrolling and cache blocking techniques to speed up stencil-based operations. Spatial derivatives are constructed using a customisable finite-difference formulation.

## LightKrylov: Abstract linear algebra

|   |   |
|---|---|
| **{{< fa brands github size=1em >}} repository** | [`LightKrylov`](https://github.com/nekStab/LightKrylov) |
| **License**  | BSD 3-Clause |
| **Programming Language** | Fortran |
| **Core developers** | [J.-Ch. Loiseau](https://loiseaujc.github.io/) and [Simon Kern](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simon-kern-49600382/?originalSubdomain=fr) |

`LightKrylov` is a modern Fortran package for linear algebra based on Krylov methods. It leverages Fortran's `abstract type` feature to provide generic implementations of the various Krylov methods. The only requirement from the user to benefit from the capabilities of `LightKrylov` is to extend the `abstract_vector` and `abstract_linop` types to define their notion of vectors and linear operators. `LightKrylov` then provides the following functionalities:

- Krylov factorizations: `arnoldi`, `lanczos`, `bidiagonalization`.
- Spectral analysis: `eigs`, `eighs`, `svds`.
- Linear systems: `gmres`, `cg`.
- Nonlinear system: `newton`.

To date, `LightKrylov` can handle real and complex-valued vectors and linear operators, using both single and double precision arithmetic. This was made possible thanks to [`fypp`](https://github.com/aradi/fypp), a python powered Fortran meta programming utility.
